safetyNotes: Safety profile depends on species identity, preparation quality, dose, and co-medications; start low, avoid prolonged high-dose use without supervision, and stop if adverse effects occur.

sources.title: PubMed search: ginger

description: Ginger is a high-utility herb commonly chosen for digestive comfort and food-integrated daily routines.
dosage: Food-use amounts and concentrated supplement servings differ; follow product-specific labels for capsules/extracts.
duration: Used both as-needed and in recurring daily routines depending on goal and tolerance.
therapeuticUses: Simple foundational herb stacking with turmeric or peppermint
categoryUseContext: Most often used for digestive support, post-meal comfort, and practical kitchen-to-supplement crossover use.
evidenceLevel: Moderate evidence in selected digestive and nausea-related contexts; form and concentration differences matter.
